_comment: FAITHFUL logistics magnitudes for The Campaign for North Africa, transcribed from the rulebook charts (docs/rules/90-charts-tables-and-play-aids.md; garbled tables cross-checked against the scanned PDF pages 108-109). This mirrors data/unit_stats.json: raw rulebook values plus a _comment citing the exact chart/rule for each. It is a DATA FILE ONLY -- the engine is not wired to it in this phase. Where a value is noted PROXY/ABSTRACTED, the rulebook itself gives no single number for the land game. Each block also records the current engine proxy (game/supply.py, game/scenario.py, game/oob.py, game/engine.py) so the wire step can measure the rescale. See scale_observation in the task report.

point_definitions._comment: The physical scale of one supply Point, for sanity-checking the equivalent-weights conversions. Fuel [49.11], Ammo [50.11], plus the [54.5] tonnages.

fuel_consumption._comment: [49.19] FUEL CONSUMPTION CHART + rules 49.13/49.14. LAW: a TOE Strength Point of vehicles burns (fuel_consumption_rate) Fuel Points for every 5 CP -- or fraction -- expended IN MOVEMENT (49.13); combat CP burn no fuel (49.13). Total for a unit = rate x ceil(CP/5) x TOE_strength_points. Foot/camel/motorcycle burn nothing (49.12). The [49.19] grid below is that multiplication table (rows=CP expended, cols=rate 1..6, cell=Fuel Points per TOE point); sub-5-CP rows are fractional (0.2..4.8) and the game tracks them as fractions. ENGINE PROXY (game/supply.py fuel_rate/_FUEL_RATE_PROXY + fuel_cost): rate is a flat per-mobility-class 2(VEHICLE)/1(else), and fuel_cost = rate x ceil(CP/5) with NO x strength multiply -- so the engine (a) uses rate 2 where real tanks are 3-7, and (b) omits the per-TOE-point factor, undercharging a full-strength unit by ~strength-fold. faithful law is present; only the magnitude/units are proxy.
fuel_consumption.fuel_capacity_rating_formula: CPA * (1/5) * fuel_consumption_rate   [49.14]  -- the fuel a TOE point carries in its own tanks

chart_49_19._comment: rows are CP's expended in movement; columns keyed by the vehicle's fuel consumption rate 1..6; value = Fuel Points consumed per TOE Strength Point.

fuel_consumption_rate_by_model._comment: The per-model 'Fuel Rate' column of the Tank & Gun Characteristics Charts ([4.47] Commonwealth, [4.48] Italian, [4.49] German) -- this is the '1..6' index into chart_49_19. Guns are almost all rate 1; SP guns 2-3; tanks 1-7. These belong on the unit/model and mirror data/unit_stats.json 'models'. WIRED: game/logistics_data.py fuel_rate_by_model() flattens the three tank sub-dicts onto Unit.fuel_rate (game/oob.py); the gun/SP scalars are oob role defaults. Under the Regime-B FULL-LOGISTICS fuel law (49.13, game/supply.py fuel_cost) the rate is multiplied by ceil(CP/5) x TOE-strength.

evaporation_49_3._comment: [49.3] Fuel evaporation/spillage, applied to ALL on-map fuel (not convoys at sea) during the Stores Expenditure Stage each Game-Turn. Water evaporates the same way (52.44).

ammunition_consumption._comment: [50.2] AMMUNITION CONSUMPTION RATES TABLE (recovered from PDF page 108 -- did not OCR into docs/rules/90). LAW (50.14): Ammo Points expended = per-function rate x the number of TOE Strength Points USING that combat function. A 4-TOE 150mm gun barraging spends 4x4=16 Ammo regardless of actual barrage points (50.14). Two sub-tables: the full 'Logistics Game Played' rates and the coarser 'Logistics Game Abstracted' battalion-equivalent rates. The engine models the LAND game, so use the 'played' land rates. ENGINE PROXY (game/supply.py AMMO_RATE): barrage 4 (FAITHFUL), anti_armor 2 (should be 3), assault 1 (should be 2); AA not modelled.

logistics_game_played._comment: Ammo Points per TOE Strength Point committed to the function.
logistics_game_played.close_assault_applies_to: Armor-class, Gun-class, MG-infantry, Heavy-Weapon-infantry (the only close-assault classes listed on the chart)

logistics_game_abstracted._comment: Coarse battalion-equivalent rates for the abstracted logistics game (NOT what the land engine uses; recorded for completeness).

captured_ammo_usable_fraction._comment: [50.16] only 1/3 (rounded up) of captured/recaptured Ammo Points are usable by the captor; the rest are lost.

stores_consumption._comment: Rule 51.1 (no numbered chart; the rates are inline in chapter 51). Stores are distributed once at the START of each Game-Turn (not per OpStage). ENGINE PROXY (game/supply.py stores_cost): 4 per TOE for combat units, 1 per TOE for non-combat -- FAITHFUL for the combat/HQ split; guards, prisoners, and half-rations are not yet modelled.

shortfall_51_22_attrition._comment: [51.22] For every 2 CONSECUTIVE Game-Turns without stores, lose 2% of TOE Strength Points (rounded to nearest whole), progressive/cumulative: 2% at turn 2, 4% at turn 4, 6% at turn 6... INFANTRY-type TOE points only; guns and tanks exempt (51.22).

water._comment: Chapter 52. Consumption rates are inline (52.41/52.42/52.43/52.6); the [52.7] WATER AVAILABILITY TABLE gives WELL YIELDS. ENGINE PROXY (game/supply.py water_cost): 1 flat for infantry, 1 per TOE for vehicle/gun/truck -- FAITHFUL to 52.41/52.42; hot-weather +1 is a PROXY (52.43 gives no number). Pasta Point handled in game/engine.py _pasta_point (52.6).

consumption_52_4.infantry_note: 1 Water Point per Operations Stage REGARDLESS of TOE strength [52.41]
consumption_52_4.vehicle_note: 1 Water per TOE point per OpStage IF it uses any of its CPA [52.42]
consumption_52_4.hot_weather_addition: UNSPECIFIED -- 52.43 says 'requires additional water usage' but charts no number; engine proxies +1
consumption_52_4.pasta_note: each Italian battalion must receive +1 Water Point when Stores are distributed [52.6]

availability_table_52_7._comment: [52.7] WATER AVAILABILITY TABLE -- Water Points drawn from a well by die roll. A '*' entry: after drawing, roll one die secretly; on a 1 the Town/Bir is Depleted. Oases and Major Cities never deplete and yield unlimited water.

engine_proxy.water_cost_formula: (strength if vehicle/gun/truck else 1) + (1 if hot else 0)

supply_dump_capacity_54_12._comment: [54.12] SUPPLY DUMP CAPACITY CHART -- the MAXIMUM Points of each commodity a dump may hold, by location/terrain. 'U' = unlimited. Non-Dump = the ambient capacity of any non-dump hex. THIS IS THE BIG SCALE GAP: real dumps hold thousands of points; the engine seeds 40/60/120/150-point proxy dumps (game/oob.py DUMP_*). A faithful rescale multiplies dump seeds by ~20x (village stores) up to ~80x (other-terrain fuel). See scale_observation.

tunis_tripoli_offmap_box.note: boxes may NOT themselves be used as supply dumps

supply_dump_demolition_54_17._comment: [54.17] SUPPLY DUMP DEMOLITION TABLE -- percentage of a dump's supplies destroyed by die (modified). Included for completeness; not a consumption rate. Modifiers are cumulative (see rulebook 54.17).

truck_characteristics_54_2._comment: [54.2] TRUCK CHARACTERISTICS CHART (MAXIMUM TRANSPORT CAPACITY). Per Truck Point: CPA when motorizing infantry/guns/supplies; how many TOE Strength Points of each class it can carry; how many SUPPLY POINTS of each commodity it can carry in convoy; its own Fuel Capacity; a Fuel Consumption Factor; and its Breakdown Adjustment Rating (BAR, in left column-shifts). na = not allowed. Light infantry needs 2 Light Truck Points per infantry TOE point (the '1/2' entry). BAR '2L' = 2 left shifts; the light-truck '2L' has a footnote that off-road it accrues +1 breakdown point. NOT YET IN ENGINE (truck haulage is CHUNK 4); game/supply.py currently carries supply units as flat MOTORIZED points (SUPPLY_CPA=15, SUPPLY_MOVE_FUEL=1).

toe_capacity.infantry_note: requires 2 Light Truck Points per 1 infantry TOE point

equivalent_weights_54_5._comment: [54.5] EQUIVALENT WEIGHTS CHART -- tonnage of one Point of each commodity, used to convert convoy/rail/air/interport TONNAGE (e.g. the [56.5] convoy capacity) into supply Points. So 1 ton = 1/4 Ammo pt = 8 Fuel pts = 1 Stores pt = 6 Water pts. Replacement Points and Truck/Motorization Points shipped by Axis Naval Convoy have VARIABLE tonnage -- 'see Axis Replacement Pool' (not a fixed chart value).

replacement_point_tons.air_note: only infantry-class replacement points may go by air

ports_55_3._comment: [55.3] PORT CAPACITY AND EFFICIENCY LEVEL CHART. Per Operations Stage a port ships 'in'/'out' that many STACKING POINTS. Efficiency Level = damage the port absorbs; losing one level cuts capacity by a fraction of 1/level. Stacking-points handled and tonnage handled are independent; Axis-convoy REPLACEMENT points off-load by the stacking-point schedule, not tonnage. Reinforcements are never gated by port capacity/efficiency. Special: Bizerta unusable by Axis before Game-Turn 35 (then roll to activate); Tobruk begins BELOW its listed efficiency 5 because the scuttled San Giorgio partially blocks the harbour; Bardia/Sollum in=0 but any one 0-stacking-point unit may still be shipped in. Port UNBLOCK costs (54.x demolition/repair table): Tobruk 25 Ammo + 10 Stores per level; Benghazi 100 Ammo + 50 Stores; other ports 50 Ammo + 25 Stores. ENGINE PROXY: game/scenario.py TOBRUK_PORT_CAP {ammo 300, fuel 100, stores 500, water 150} + eff 2/5, rear ports one Supply-Unit load -- the real chart is in STACKING POINTS not commodity points, so the engine's per-commodity port caps are a re-derivation, not the chart.

tobruk.note: starts below eff 5 -- San Giorgio partially blocks harbour

bardia.note: any one 0-SP unit may still ship in

sollum.note: any one 0-SP unit may still ship in

port_supply_tonnage_55_3._comment: [55.3] PORT CAPACITY AND EFFICIENCY LEVEL CHART -- the MAXIMUM TONNAGE (supply) column, read directly from the scanned chart (PDF page 138/139, book-chart page 15; this column did NOT survive OCR into ports_55_3.efficiency_and_stacking, which only carried the Stacking-Point in/out schedule). Per 55.13/55.14 this is the tonnage of supplies (fuel/ammo/stores/water) a port may ship IN and/or OUT per Operations Stage AT PEAK EFFICIENCY; convert tonnage<->Points via equivalent_weights_54_5. A port reduced to Efficiency Level L of max M ships M/L -- i.e. L/M -- of this tonnage, rounded UP to the nearest ton (55.14; Benghazi example: 2500 t, max eff 3, reduced to 1 => 834 t). The rulebook stresses the stacking-point and tonnage schedules are INDEPENDENT (they do not gate each other), and that Axis-convoy replacement points off-load by the stacking-point schedule, not tonnage. All values below read cleanly; none are FLAGGED. efficiency_level is the same rating carried in ports_55_3.efficiency_and_stacking and is repeated here so the 55.14 tonnage-reduction math is self-contained.

tobruk.note: starts below eff 5 -- San Giorgio partially blocks harbour

port_supply_tonnage_55_3.reduction_rule_55_14: at efficiency level L of maximum M, tonnage = ceil(max_tons * L / M)

axis_naval_convoys_56._comment: The Axis Naval Convoy System (chapter 56). Convoy CAPACITY is a TONNAGE that must be converted to supply Points via [54.5]. Replacement Points and Truck/Motorization Points shipped by convoy draw VARIABLE tonnage from the Axis Replacement Pool (see [54.5] and 56 -- there is NO fixed per-point tonnage for those). ENGINE PROXY: game/scenario.py _rommel_convoys ships whole Supply-Unit loads / port-loads on a static timetable; the 56.4 monthly level and 56.5 tonnage-by-die are not modelled.

convoy_level_chart_56_4._comment: [56.4] AXIS NAVAL CONVOY LEVEL CHART -- the convoy Level letter (A-G) in effect each month; feeds into [56.5]. '-' = no convoys that month.

convoy_capacity_table_56_5._comment: [56.5] AXIS NAVAL CONVOY CAPACITY TABLE -- total tonnage = fixed_tons + variable_tons_per_die * die_roll(1-6), rounded UP to nearest 1,000 tons. Convert to supply Points via equivalent_weights_54_5.

air_convoy_bombing_crt_41_66._comment: [41.66]/[32.66] the AXIS NAVAL CONVOY row of the [41.5] AIR BOMBARDMENT AND SECONDARY BARRAGE TARGETS TABLE, transcribed cell-by-cell from the scanned foldout chart page 12 (PDF page-107; this dense grid did NOT OCR into docs/rules/90 -- see the page-108 sibling that only carried the Key). Procedure (41.66): the Commonwealth Player totals his attacking Bomb Points, selects the matching bomb_points column, rolls two dice reading them SEQUENTIALLY as a two-digit code (tens=first die, units=second die: 11,12..16,21..66), and reads the cross-indexed result -- expressed in TENS OF PERCENT of the cargo in that convoy lost. 41.67 then divides that percentage evenly among the cargo types, rounding each LOST amount up. Each column's die entries partition all 36 sequential codes exactly (verified). Columns with bomb_points below their floor never damage a convoy (the 1..20 column is a flat 0%). hi=null on the final column is the open 471+ bracket. The three torpedo/barrage-weapon header rows of the same chart are NOT transcribed (torpedoes 41.7 and the barrage-vs-facility path 12.53 are deferred); only the Bomb-Points columns feed convoy bombing.

air_distance_chart_56_18._comment: [56.18] AXIS NAVAL CONVOY AIR DISTANCE CHART -- distance in hexes from an air-base box (Sicily/Italy/Crete/Malta/Benghazi/Derna/Tobruk) to each shipping lane's Rally Point, for interdiction range checks. Not a logistics MAGNITUDE (it gates air missions against convoys); recorded as a reference only. Lanes: 1 Sicily->Bizerta, 2 Sicily->Tripoli, 3 Italy->Benghazi, 4 Greece->Benghazi, 5 Greece->Tobruk, 6 Italy->Tobruk. Full grid in docs/rules/90 lines ~173-188.

air_bombardment_41_5._comment: [41.5] AIR BOMBARDMENT AND SECONDARY BARRAGE TARGETS TABLE, the PORTS row (shared with Airfields / Air Landing Strips), transcribed and eyes-verified cell-by-cell from the scanned foldout chart page 12 (PDF page 107) -- the dense grid that did NOT OCR into docs/rules/90 (only the row labels came through, lines ~1005/1025). Procedure (41.22 / 41.39B): the bombing Player totals his Bomb Points, selects the matching bomb_points column, rolls two dice reading them SEQUENTIALLY as a two-digit code (tens=first die, units=second die: 11,12..16,21..66), and reads the cross-indexed result -- the number of Efficiency LEVELS the port loses (55.1). A result of 0 is No Effect: the port lost no levels this OpStage, so 55.18 lets it regenerate. Each column's die entries partition all 36 sequential codes exactly (guarded by tests/test_air.py). hi=null on the final column is the open 471+ bracket. The Axis Naval Convoy row of this SAME chart lives in axis_naval_convoys_56.air_convoy_bombing_crt_41_66 (already transcribed and tested); the remaining land rows (Supply Dump, Fortification, Railroad, Road, Trucks/Combat Units) are the broader T0-10 job and are not transcribed here.

ports._row: Airfields / Air Landing Strips / Ports -- Efficiency Levels lost (41.39B Bombing Ports)

scenario_61_desert_fox_initial_supply._comment: Initial supply placement and port states for the Desert Fox scenario group (docs/rules/61-scenario-group-two-the-desert-fox.md). DATA ONLY: the engine's game/scenario.py currently seeds proxy dumps/ports (see supply_dump_capacity_54_12.engine_proxy_dump_seeds and ports_55_3.engine_proxy). The percentage figures are PLACEMENT constraints on how the pool is split across dumps, not consumption rates. Line numbers cite the current markdown transcription.

axis_dump_split_61_44._comment: [61.44] Supply (docs/rules/61 line 196): the Axis Player divides this pool among his THREE ACTIVE dumps (he receives five dumps total, two of which are Dummies -- line 194); no more than 50% of each commodity may sit at any one dump.

axis_tripoli_builtin_61_44._comment: [61.44] (line 198): Tripoli box built-in supplies, in addition to the dump split above.

commonwealth_dump_split_61_36._comment: [61.36] Supply (line 112): the Commonwealth Player divides this pool among his NINE Dumps (he receives nine Dumps plus three Dummy Dumps -- line 104); no more than 25% of each item at any one dump, and every ACTUAL dump must hold at least 50 of each item.

commonwealth_tobruk_builtin_61_36._comment: [61.36] (line 110): Tobruk built-in supplies (Tobruk is itself a supply dump -- line 104), in addition to the dump split.

commonwealth_benghazi_builtin_61_36._comment: [61.36] (line 108): Benghazi built-in supplies (Benghazi is also a supply dump); Benghazi additionally holds 10 Points of Italian Prisoners, a POW Camp, and one Commonwealth Guard.

initial_port_states_61_6._comment: [61.6] Construction on the map at start (line 210): all ports begin at their full (listed 55.3) Efficiency Level EXCEPT the two below -- Benghazi scuttled to zero, Tobruk at seven with the San Giorgio still blocking the harbour. FLAG: Tobruk's scenario start of 7 exceeds the 55.3 listed maximum of 5 (and the -3 San Giorgio penalty per 55.25 is unaccounted for); transcribed faithfully verbatim as the rulebook states it -- do not silently reconcile.

scenario_60_campaign_initial_supply._comment: [60.34]/[60.44] initial supply pools for the FULL CAMPAIGN (Scenario Group One, docs/rules/60-scenario-group-one-the-italians.md). Rule 64.3 mandates SECTION 60 for the entire campaign's initial placement; the engine previously mis-seeded the SECTION-61 Desert Fox pools above (game/oob.py), handing the Axis 9600 Fuel where 60.34 charts 3000. These are the ANONYMOUS FIELD-DUMP pools only (the 'Dump 1/2/I' counters the player places on the map); the NAMED-location dumps (Tobruk, Bardia, Benghazi, Derna, Mersa Matruh, Sidi Barrani) are seeded elsewhere in game/scenario.py. The airfield-distributed pools (Axis 1200 ammo/850 fuel/100 stores/100 water; CW 200/250/50) belong to the Air Game and are not seeded here.

axis_field_dump_pool_60_34._comment: [60.34] (docs/rules/60 lines 133-134): the sum of the two anonymous field dumps -- Dump 1 (1000 ammo/1500 fuel/1500 stores/200 water) + Dump 2 (1000/1500/1000/200), placed on map section C in Libya. Two additional Dummy dumps carry no supply.

commonwealth_field_dump_pool_60_44._comment: [60.44] (docs/rules/60 line 229): Dump I (500 ammo/750 fuel/500 stores), the one anonymous field dump placed in Egypt on map C or D. One additional Dummy dump carries no supply. 60.44 charts NO dump Water for the Commonwealth -- forward CW water is the 52.7 wells / 54.3 rail (both deferred); Cairo and Alexandria hold unlimited supply, water included (60.44 note).
